mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the equity discount rate and reduces financing stress, so when policy is still at the effective lower bound or moving down, S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are positive.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of periods with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration above policy rates signals imminent tightening and margin pressure; when year-over-year CPI growth exceeds the federal funds rate, equity multiples compress and the next 20 trading days are negative.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation rate rises above FEDFUNDS by more than 1.0 percentage point
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes deliver positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the sign of the spread does not improve directional accuracy versus either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE identifies weakening household income and softer demand expectations, so when unemployment is increasing over a short window, the S&P 500 tends to fall over the next 5 trading days as analysts reprice near-term growth.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if, after the trigger, 5-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of cases or the signal's hit rate is indistinguishable from the unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in PAYEMS and decline in UNRATE indicates broadening payroll demand and falling slack, which supports earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform PAYEMS-alone and UNRATE-alone models, or if triggered joint episodes fail to deliver a positive average 20-day forward return.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in RSAFS indicates softer nominal final demand and weaker revenue growth for listed firms, so sustained retail sales weakness predicts negative S&P 500 performance over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not lower following 3-month declines in RSAFS, or if the rolling 3-year sign accuracy falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A rising INDPRO together with falling UNRATE signals real activity expanding faster than labor slack, a combination that supports cyclical earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint signal is not stronger than each single series, or if episodes with rising INDPRO and falling UNRATE do not outperform the unconditional 20-day baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in HOUST marks demand-sensitive housing acceleration that leads broader risk appetite, so when housing starts rebound from depressed levels the S&P 500 posts positive returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises more than 10% from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered rebounds do not produce above-baseline 60-day forward SPX returns, or if the sign effect disappears after two consecutive monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a simultaneous rise in CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS indicates policy lag behind inflation, which depresses valuation multiples and makes S&P 500 returns negative over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises while CPIAUCSL inflation remains above 3% annualized
failure_condition: Reject if tightening-and-high-inflation episodes do not generate negative 5-day forward returns, or if the joint condition fails to beat either CPIAUCSL or FEDFUNDS alone in out-of-sample hit rate.
